trivia_bullets.text: Four printings: Core Set 2020 (first print, 2019-07-12), Theros Beyond Death (2020-01-24), Strixhaven Mystical Archive English (2021-04-23, art by Benjamin Ee) and Japanese alternate art (2021-04-23, art by Yangyang & Xiaji), Foundations Jumpstart (2024-11-15).
sources: Scryfall: all prints via oracle_id search

trivia_bullets.text: "Renata launched a dozen arrows into the minotaur's thick hide, but the monster didn't slow its charge. The fury of Mogis was upon it." — Luphea of Setessa, Histories. Second Luphea quote in this batch (also on Careless Celebrant), establishing Luphea's Histories as a deliberate flavor-cycle source text in THB. Mogis is the twin of Iroas and patron deity of minotaurs.
sources: MTG Wiki: Mogis via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Hipsters of the Coast common review grade D: 'Red's strictly worse version of Giant Growth.' Consistent Pauper/PauperCommander application as a 1-mana pump. Legal in Pauper.
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: THB Common Review 2020-01

trivia_bullets.text: THB printing illustrated by Tomasz Jedruszek. The Strixhaven Mystical Archive Japanese alternate art (Yangyang & Xiaji) gave the card a prestige collector profile above its commons-tier gameplay reputation.

related_cards.name: Mogis, God of Slaughter
related_cards.note: Named god whose divine fury is described in this card's flavor text

related_cards.name: Infuriate
related_cards.note: English Mystical Archive alternate art (Benjamin Ee, no. 41)

related_cards.name: Skophos Warleader
related_cards.note: Fellow Mogis-worshipping minotaur in same set
